title: SA signs US$1.5 billion loan with World Bank
description: NATIONAL NEWS - The South African government...
bodyText: [{"type":"paragraph","text":"NATIONAL NEWS - The South African government and the World Bank have signed a US$1.5 billion Development Policy Loan Agreement that will assist in unlocking key infrastructure bottlenecks, particularly in the energy and freight transport sectors.","html":"NATIONAL NEWS - The South African government and the World Bank have signed a US$1.5 billion Development Policy Loan Agreement that will assist in unlocking key infrastructure bottlenecks, particularly in the energy and freight transport sectors."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"In a statement on Monday, the National Treasury explained that the loan is aimed at supporting critical structural reforms to enhance the efficiency, resilience, and sustainability of the country’s infrastructure services."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The loan support is anchored on three key pillars of structural reform: improving energy security, enhancing the efficiency and competitiveness of freight transport services, and supporting South Africa’s transition toward a low carbon economy.","html":"The loan support is anchored on three key pillars of structural reform: improving energy security, enhancing the efficiency and competitiveness of freight transport services, and supporting South Africa’s transition toward a low carbon economy."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"These reforms are critical enablers of inclusive growth and job creation.","html":"These reforms are critical enablers of inclusive growth and job creation."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“This partnership marks a significant step towards addressing South Africa’s pressing economic challenges of low growth and high unemployment.","html":"“This partnership marks a significant step towards addressing South Africa’s pressing economic challenges of low growth and high unemployment."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“The financing forms part of the government’s broader efforts to implement structural reforms that strengthen public institutions, crowd in private investment, and improve service delivery across priority sectors of the economy,” National Treasury said.","html":"“The financing forms part of the government’s broader efforts to implement structural reforms that strengthen public institutions, crowd in private investment, and improve service delivery across priority sectors of the economy,” National Treasury said."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The financing terms of the loan are in line with National Treasury’s financing strategy.","html":"The financing terms of the loan are in line with National Treasury’s financing strategy."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Specifically, the loan offers both favourable interest rates and flexible repayment terms, contributing to minimising increase in debt service costs.","html":"Specifically, the loan offers both favourable interest rates and flexible repayment terms, contributing to minimising increase in debt service costs."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"The financing terms of the World Bank loan are as follows:"},{"type":"list","items":["Nominal value: US$1.5 billion,","Maturity: 16 years with a 3 year-grace period,","Interest rate: 6-month Secured Overnight Financing Rate (SOFR) plus 1.49%."],"ordered":false},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“The National Treasury wishes to express its appreciation to the World Bank for its continued partnership and support in advancing South Africa’s development objectives. This agreement reinforces the strong and constructive collaboration between the World Bank and the government of South Africa.” - SAnews.gov.za","html":"“The National Treasury wishes to express its appreciation to the World Bank for its continued partnership and support in advancing South Africa’s development objectives. This agreement reinforces the strong and constructive collaboration between the World Bank and the government of South Africa.” - SAnews.gov.za"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’","html":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’"}]

images.alt: SA signs US$1.5 billion loan with World Bank
